title: Improving Medication Adherence for Caribbean Patients with HIV

content.text: Addressing Barriers to Adherence in the Caribbean

content.body: Medication adherence is a significant challenge for Caribbean patients living with HIV. Factors like high patient-to-staff ratios, transportation difficulties, stigma, and lack of family support can all impact adherence. Pharmacy technicians play a crucial role in identifying and addressing these barriers.

In many Caribbean countries, HIV remains highly stigmatized. Patients may be reluctant to disclose their status or seek care due to fear of discrimination. Pharmacy technicians can provide a non-judgmental, confidential environment to discuss adherence challenges.
content.title: Stigma and Disclosure

content.body: Although antiretroviral therapy is available through national health systems across the Caribbean, out-of-pocket costs for lab tests, transportation, and missed work can be barriers. Pharmacy technicians can connect patients to financial assistance programs and community resources.
content.title: Medication Costs and Access

content.definition: A strategy where a healthcare worker or community member observes the patient taking their medication, used to improve adherence in some Caribbean nations like Jamaica and Guyana.
